hero.imageAlt: Aerial view of Sømliøya in winter light
hero.title: Sømliøya
hero.subtitle: Where the world becomes quieter

children.text: Sømliøya is a small private island in the Nærøysund municipality of Trøndelag, on the west coast of Norway. It sits at the mouth of a narrow fjord, surrounded by open sea to the west and mountains to the east.

The island has been in the same family for generations. It is a place shaped by the rhythms of the sea and the seasons — where the light changes dramatically between winter and summer, and where the silence is deep and uninterrupted.
We rent it to groups who want to step outside their ordinary lives for a while. For a day, a weekend, or a week — the island becomes yours.

There is room for up to 20 people across two houses. There is a sauna, a firepit, kayaks, fishing rods, and a kitchen big enough for communal cooking. But mostly there is space — physical and mental — to slow down.

pullQuote: It is a place where you can step outside the world for a while.

children.text: An interactive 3D model of Sømliøya rendered in real time from satellite data. Fly over the fjords, explore the coastline.

firstImage.alt: Mirror-still fjord at sunset with mountains

secondImage.alt: Dramatic orange and pink sunset over the Norwegian fjord
